source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: executeWithdraw may be blocked if any of the users are blacklisted from the baseToken
summary: Some tokens (e.g. USDC, USDT) have a contract level admin controlled address blocklist. If an address is blocked, then transfers to and from that address are forbidden.
root_cause: permissionless denial-of-service condition in executeWithdraw may be blocked if any of the users are blacklisted from the baseToken, allowing one actor to block a required user or protocol operation
broken_invariants: user exit paths must remain available and value-preserving under the root cause: permissionless denial-of-service condition in executeWithdraw may be blocked if any of the users are blacklisted from the baseToken, allowing one actor to block a required user or protocol operation
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
privileged, owner, and license checks must be enforced on every value-moving path, not only on the nominal entry point
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Some tokens (e.g. USDC, USDT) have a contract level admin controlled address blocklist. If an address is blocked, then transfers to and from that address are forbidden.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Validate that the receiver is not blacklisted before making the ERC20::safeTransfer call, whenever the baseToken has a contract level admin controlled address blocklist (e.g. USDC, USDT). function executeWithdraw(uint256 maxIterations) public onlyManager nonReentrant whenNotPaused { ... while ( currentWithdrawGroup.lastId > firstTemp && withdrawQueue.queue[firstTemp].calculationTime + withdrawWaitingTime <= block.timestamp // @audit-info use withdrawRequest time && i < maxIterations ) { i += 1; WithdrawRequest memory data = withdrawQueue.queue[firstTemp]; uint256 shares = data.shares; // calculate the base token amount that the user will receive based on the total available amount uint256 baseTokenAmount = data.amount * currentWithdrawGroup.totalABAmount / currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmountFullfilled; withdrawRequestsByAddress[data.owner] -= shares; _burn(data.owner, shares); processedBaseTokenAmount += data.amount; { uint256 feeAmount = baseTokenAmount * withdrawFee / FEE_PRECISION; withdrawFeeAmount += feeAmount; baseTokenAmount = baseTokenAmount - feeAmount; } + if (baseToken.isBlacklisted(data.receiver)) { + continue; + } baseToken.safeTransfer(data.receiver, baseTokenAmount);
